S.text: Established patient presents for annual comprehensive eye exam; also due for updated spectacles.
Reports gradual distance blur over ~2 years, worse driving at night with difficulty reading road signs.
Mild end-of-day eyestrain with ~8 hours of computer use; denies headaches.
Family history: mother with dry eye; no known serious ocular disease.

O.text: Pupils equal and reactive, no afferent pupillary defect. Confrontation fields full OU.
Manifest refraction OD -3.75 -0.75 x 170, VA cc 20/20 OD.
Manifest refraction OS -4.25 -1.00 x 010, VA cc 20/20 OS. Mild myopic shift (~0.50 D) since prior.
Slit lamp: lids/lashes clean, conjunctiva white and quiet, corneas clear, anterior chambers deep and quiet, lenses clear OU with no cataract.
Dilated fundus exam: optic nerves healthy, C/D 0.3 OU with healthy pink rim tissue.
Maculae flat and healthy without drusen, vessels normal caliber, peripheral retina intact 360 OU without tears or holes.

A.text: Myopia with astigmatism OU; mild myopic progression since prior exam.
Ocular health otherwise unremarkable; anterior and posterior segments healthy OU.

P.text: Update spectacle Rx with new refraction; retain anti-reflective coating for night driving.
Discussed likely future reading add given age 48; deferred at this time as patient managing at near.
RTC 1 year for next comprehensive exam; return sooner for sudden flashes or a visual field curtain.
